title: Trace.Write and Trace.WriteLine should be avoided
description: "Trace.Write" and "Trace.WriteLine" should not be usedAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityCode SmellloggingWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoTrace.WriteandTrace.WriteLinemethods are writing to the underlying output stream directly, bypassing the trace formatting and filtering performed byTraceListener.TraceEventimplementations. It is preferred to useTrace.TraceError,Trace.TraceWarning, andTrace.TraceInformationmethods instead because they call theTraceEvent methodwhich filters the trace output according to theTraceEventType(Error, Warning or Information) and enhance the output with additional information.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-p
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Trace.Write and Trace.WriteLine bypass trace formatting and filtering. Use Trace.TraceError, Trace.TraceWarning, or Trace.TraceInformation instead.

logic.reasoning: Trace.Write and Trace.WriteLine write directly to the output stream, bypassing TraceListener.TraceEvent filtering and formatting. The preferred methods are Trace.TraceError, Trace.TraceWarning, and Trace.TraceInformation which respect TraceEventType filtering.
